grounding_note: Row 56 (POA for aging father asking about guardianship/conservatorship) split humans between Conservatorship/Guardianship and Power of Attorney. The dividing fact is capacity and consent: an elder who can still knowingly sign documents needs a POA; one who cannot needs a court-appointed guardian/conservator. Families know the capacity facts precisely and rarely state them up front.

scenarios.opening_query: I need to take over my father's finances before he loses everything. Where do I start?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney || Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship
scenarios.hidden_fact: His dementia is advanced — his doctor says he no longer understands documents, so he can't sign anything voluntarily.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His dementia is advanced — the doctor says he can't understand documents anymore, so he can't sign anything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the parent still has legal capacity to sign planning documents
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's still sharp and completely agrees — he wants to sign whatever gives me authority to help.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's still sharp and on board — happy to sign whatever gives me authority.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 56's boundary exactly: capacity+consent → POA; incapacity → court guardianship.

scenarios.opening_query: We think my mom needs a guardian. She's been making terrible decisions with her money.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her doctors say she's fully competent — just impulsive — and she's willing to sign a power of attorney letting me co-manage things.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her doctors say she's fully competent, just impulsive — and she'll sign a power of attorney so I can help manage things.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the parent is legally incapacitated or consents to voluntary arrangements
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Two doctors have documented that she can no longer manage her affairs, and she refuses all help.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Two doctors documented she can't manage her affairs anymore — and she refuses all help.
scenarios.vetting_note: Courts reject guardianship over competent adults; capacity evidence is the routing fact both ways.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother has schizophrenia and is in crisis again. Our family needs some legal structure to help him.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Criminal Law > Mental Commitment
scenarios.hidden_fact: He was picked up on a crisis hold Tuesday and the county filed for involuntary commitment — the hearing is Friday and he wants to fight it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's on a crisis hold and the county filed for commitment — hearing's Friday and he wants to fight it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an involuntary commitment proceeding is underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No commitment case — he's home but can't manage medications or money, and his psychiatrist supports a guardianship petition.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No commitment case — he's home but can't manage meds or money, and his psychiatrist supports guardianship.
scenarios.vetting_note: Commitment defense vs protective guardianship are opposite postures; the pending-hearing fact decides.

scenarios.opening_query: Grandpa's neighbor has been 'helping' him with his banking and things don't add up.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: Bank records show $47,000 moved to the neighbor's account in six months, and grandpa recently signed a new will leaving the neighbor his house.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The records show $47,000 went to the neighbor in six months — and grandpa signed a new will leaving him the house.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether financial exploitation and document changes have already occurred
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No money is missing yet — we just want a proper structure in place because grandpa is slipping and too trusting.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's missing yet — we just want proper protection in place because he's slipping and too trusting.
scenarios.vetting_note: Actual exploitation vs preventive protection; the money-trail fact separates the labels.

scenarios.opening_query: The court gave my aunt control over my grandmother years ago and the family thinks it's time to revisit that.
scenarios.final: Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments
scenarios.hidden_fact: Grandma has recovered remarkably since her stroke — her doctor says she can manage again, and she wants the guardianship terminated.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Grandma's recovered since the stroke — her doctor says she can manage again, and she wants the guardianship ended.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the goal is ending an existing guardianship versus modifying it
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Grandma still needs a guardian — the issue is my aunt's spending, and we want a different guardian appointed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She still needs a guardian — the problem is my aunt's spending. We want a replacement appointed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Restoration-of-rights has its own taxonomy label; recovery evidence is the routing fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother needs long-term memory care soon and the costs we're being quoted are impossible.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Long-term Care Issues || Wills & Trusts > Medicare/Medicaid Planning || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid
scenarios.hidden_fact: We already applied for Medicaid and were denied over a five-year-old property transfer — we need the denial appealed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We already applied for Medicaid and got denied over an old property transfer — we need that denial appealed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a benefits application has already been denied (appeal) versus planning ahead
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We haven't applied for anything yet — we want her savings structured now so she can qualify when the time comes.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We haven't applied yet — we want her savings structured so she qualifies when it's time.
scenarios.vetting_note: Denied-vs-planning is the taxonomy's own boundary between Administrative Law and W&T Medicaid Planning.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm my dad's power of attorney and his care facility is questioning what I'm allowed to do.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney || Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship
scenarios.hidden_fact: The POA only covers finances — the facility needs medical decisions made and dad can no longer consent, so I'm told I need guardianship for healthcare.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The POA is financial only — the facility needs medical decisions and dad can't consent anymore, so they say I need guardianship.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the existing document's scope covers the decisions now needed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The POA is broad and valid — the facility just wants it reviewed and re-certified with current statutory language.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The POA is broad and valid — they just want it reviewed and updated to current language.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 56's user literally holds a POA and asks what it covers; scope-gap fact escalates to guardianship.

scenarios.opening_query: Social Security stopped sending my disabled son's checks to me and everything is a mess now.
scenarios.final: Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments
scenarios.hidden_fact: They appointed his group home as representative payee after a complaint — I want the payee assignment reversed and back with me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They made his group home the representative payee after a complaint — I want that payee assignment reversed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a representative-payee assignment is the thing being contested
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The checks stopped because they say he's over the resource limit — the eligibility decision itself is what's wrong.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The checks stopped over a resource-limit finding — the eligibility decision is what's wrong.
scenarios.vetting_note: Payee-assignment reversal has a dedicated W&T label distinct from benefits eligibility appeals.

scenarios.opening_query: My wife had a stroke and can't communicate. The hospital keeps asking who has authority to decide things.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney
scenarios.hidden_fact: She signed a healthcare power of attorney naming me years ago — I found it in our files; I just need it validated and accepted by the hospital.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She signed a healthcare POA naming me years ago — I found it. I just need the hospital to accept it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether valid advance documents already exist
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We never signed anything — no POA, no advance directive — so I apparently need the court to appoint me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We never signed anything — so I apparently need the court to appoint me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Existing-document discovery flips a guardianship petition into POA enforcement; families genuinely don't know until they look.

scenarios.opening_query: My parents are aging fast and I'm the only child nearby. I want to get ahead of the legal side.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > General (Wills/Trusts/Estates) || Wills & Trusts > Long-term Care Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: Dad's COPD means a care facility within two years — the real question is protecting the farm from care costs while getting him qualified for help.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Dad's COPD means a facility within a couple years — the real question is protecting the farm from care costs while qualifying him for help.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether imminent long-term-care costs are the planning driver
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're healthy — this is ordinary wills, directives, and beneficiary cleanup while everyone's well.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're healthy — it's ordinary wills and directives while everyone's well.
scenarios.vetting_note: Care-cost horizon separates long-term-care planning from generic estate planning.
